reviews.review_text:
I have to leave this review on every season out there, I am shocked.
This is an absolute masterpiece that completely redefines what interactive storytelling can be, and after finishing the entire series, I can confidently say it is the best decision-making game ever made. Telltale’s The Walking Dead isn't just a video game, it is genuinely a profound, heart-wrenching, and deeply personal journey that sets a bar so high it makes other choice-based narratives feel shallow in comparison. From the very first moment you meet Lee in the back of a police car to the final, breath-taking moments of the series, the writing remains consistently phenomenal. The way the character development evolves over multiple seasons is nothing short of legendary. You don't just play as these characters, you literally live, breathe, and struggle alongside them, feeling every ounce of their pain and every flicker of their hope. The bond you form with Clementine is arguably the greatest emotional anchor in gaming history, and watching her grow from a scared, innocent little girl into a hardened, capable, and compassionate survivor is a transformative experience for the player. The relationship between Lee and Clementine serves as the soul of the entire franchise, a masterclass in emotional pacing that teaches you about sacrifice, fatherhood, and what it means to keep your humanity when the world has lost its own. Every lesson Lee teaches her resonates through the years, making his legacy feel alive in every choice she makes long after he's gone. The supporting cast is equally incredible, every survivor you meet feels like a real person with their own histories, traumas, and hidden agendas, making the group dynamics feel authentic and often volatile. This emotional weight is constantly challenged by a rogues' gallery of terrifying villains who represent the absolute worst of what people become when society collapses. From the desperate, broken psyche of the Stranger to the chilling, calculated brutality of Carver and the fanatical leadership of Lilly, the antagonists in this series are never just "monsters", they are reflections of the different ways humans can break, making the danger feel grounded and terrifyingly real. Enhancing this entire experience is the iconic "living comic book" art style. The cel-shaded visuals pay perfect homage to the original source material, creating a gritty, atmospheric aesthetic that allows for incredibly expressive character faces. This visual choice is vital because it captures the raw emotion of a scene in a way that hyper-realism often misses, making every tear shed and every look of horror feel impactful. What makes this game stand above the rest is how it handles choice. Telltale perfected the art of the impossible situation, constantly forcing you into high-pressure scenarios where there is no "right" answer, only the lesser of two evils. These decisions weigh heavy on your soul long after you’ve turned off the screen because they don't just change a few lines of dialogue, they shape who Clementine becomes and how the world perceives your humanity. The atmosphere is oppressive and brutal, yet punctuated by moments of genuine beauty and connection that make the fight for survival feel worth it. The voice acting is top-tier, delivering performances that carry more emotional weight than most big-budget Hollywood films. Every season adds a new layer to the mythos, exploring the darkest corners of human nature and the incredible resilience of the human spirit. If you have any appreciation for narrative-driven games, morality plays, or just incredible storytelling, this is a non-negotiable must-play. It is a beautiful, devastating, and unforgettable roller coaster that will stay with you forever. 
10/10 – "Keep moving forward."
*Always stick with Kenny, do not let his character fool you, trust me.*
This game is amazing! And I thought Telltale's best game was Minecraft story mode.... I will replay this god knows how many times!

This season is considered the second best season in the game, and the best thing was in the second episode when they were eating and discovered that he ate human flesh. The episode was so enjoyable that I can't explain it.

Honestly the greatest decision based game ever made. Theres some really meaningful and tough choices in this game and it ALWAYS keeps you on your feet. The cliffhangers keep you hungry for more and EVERY decision you make youll be wondering what the other choices couldve led to. Im not sure if other people like kenny but hes my personal favorite. Theres also some very emotional moments and by the time this game was over with Id had tears in my eyes multiple times.
I dont want to say much,I dont know how to say but its the best story driven zombie game for me by far,I mean,I played the last of us,and to be honest ,I didnt like it as as much is this.

Played this 6+ Years ago after watching PewDiePie's full lets play, and still cried, played recently and cried again.

this and the last game are the best for me such a unique experience that leaves you feeling attached to characters, anxious for the quiet moments, and truly grateful when things look up. 

Its a bitter sweet story all the way through and you cant effect the plot but the relations and story can be changed and even after multiple playthroughs you can miss certain dialogue or scenes, this one in particular is great for replaying not back to back maybe, but its there and its cozy at times 
 
buy the DEF edition if you want to play them all and don't own any atm, but this game is a great standalone story.

this game is just so peak, The story and characters are amazing, the different options really makes you more immersed and careful about your decisions, the gameplay is engaging with a point and click gameplay and quick time events. This game is incredible and I highly recommend it to anyone, especially if you are on a budget (because it goes on sale for extremely cheap often) or have laptop with lackluster specs. This game is very easy to run, which is great for if your laptop or pc isnt very powerful.

What a great game i dont do reviews cuz i suck at them so yeah. 

but all the emotions this game put me through from S1-End was nuts the fact that you actually have to make hard decisions just adds something to the game that ive never seen before and it makes me feel so more immersed. i have no word other than incredible easy top 5 games of all time on my list.

Please play the game for yourself. You owe that to yourself
I wish TellTale was still a thing.

This might be the best zombie game. 
This game has shown every stage after an apocalypse, right from start of the outbreak to the death of the loved one.
One of my favorite games of all time. Really loveable characters. Incredible Protaganist. Great ending, Great story.
I love this game but the save keeps getting corrupted. The Walking Dead: The Telltale Definitive Series might be the better option here over the original.

The story is nice and all but it feels a bit slow.
I love the graphics, and the overall design of the game.
The choices are interesting in some episodes but in most of them they are boring.

This is an amazing, deeply rich, in both characters and plot, game! Definitely put me in my feels at times and I am very glad I was able to play it to experience this masterpiece! Love the graphics and style of the game as well!

U will make choices in this game that can also impacts the 2nd season (part 2) of the game making it all connected. And after finishing this game, just like the series, its not the walkers you should watch out for...its the people.

10/10

I literally cried during the ending. I will always love this game and in my top 3.
Lee, Clementine, and Kenny is a dub
Finished the game and overall peak also don't what so ever do not look up who dies it gives out spoilers.
one of my all time favorites. It's what got me into cinematic interactive fiction

Just finished The Walking Dead Season 1 today and I’m emotionally destroyed 💀
Came for a zombie game, stayed for trauma and daddy issues I didn’t ask for.
Game of the Year? More like Game of Making Me Sit in Silence After Finishing It Staring at My Screen Like 😐
Lee deserved better, Clementine deserved therapy, and I deserve compensation.
10/10 would suffer again.
то чувство когда вся игра ощущается как видео "Дааа... ♥♥♥♥♥ его в рот | На случай важных переговоров"
Best Game I Have Played in my life. i know im like 10+ years late but like holy ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥ dude, cinema.
great storyline with not too fast and not too slow pacing, all the characters are very well-written in my opinion because you can meet these kinds of people even in real life

This game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ good i was at the point where i was playing 9 hours straight it got me hooked right away im sda that this is only season 1 since if i buy the definitve version i have to replay season 1 but its a banger
Really great story, can't wait to play the entire series
id reather watch a gameplay instead of buying and playing it

THE WALKING DEAD
Score: 10/10
"Keep that hair short"
-Lee Everett

Gameplay: 10/10
Art: 10/10
Music: 10/10
Experience: 10/10
Story: 10/10

This is the best written story of any video game ever. Not once have I ever played a more incredible and compelling game with the best characters of anything I've ever seen in my life. The way this game has affected me, since I played it so many years ago as a kid, and now replaying it again and remembering why this might be my all time favorite game. I urge everyone to play this as soon as they can. Nothing will ever hit me the same way this has.

This game is so fckn stupid and so fckn bad jesus christ, the writing is horrendous nothing makes sense and nothing is even remotely realistic in their reactions, the voice acting somewhere there as well, not to mention the developing is so stupid. The artstyle isn't my favorite either, you'd expect a simplistic style to at least be somewhat of a high quality if it focuses on the stylization of it but it's weirdly grainy as well

I've been waiting to play this for so long and finally got the chance. Absolutely get this and play if you can, the storyline is amazing. I got pissed off so many times and lowkey shed a tear at the end.
May God Himself strike me down if I EVER play this game again. It's too good and heartWRENCHING

Man what can I say that hasn't already been said. This is just a masterclass in storytelling. I never read the comics or really even enjoyed the show past glenn's death (RIP to my boy) but this game was amazing from start to finish. Lee is such a compelling character to follow and clementine is the sweetest. The decisions you make can drastically change your playthrough experience, some good and bad, but the end all be all is that teltale was in their ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ bag with this game, and I don't think they're honestly ever gonna make anything this good again.

The Walking Dead is one of the best story games that I’ve ever played, the comic-book graphics are amazing and even though the game is old it still looks really good today because of the unique art style. The game optimisation is also really good because nearly any PC or console can run it smoothly. In my opinion The Walking Dead is much better than most zombie games because instead of focusing only on action it focuses on characters, emotions and the choices you make throughout the story. I love how every decision feels important and how the game introduces so many interesting characters, enemies and emotional moments that make you care about what happens next. The gameplay is simple but really fun because you get to explore areas, talk to characters, make choices and complete quick-time events during intense scenes. I also love how the game keeps track of your decisions and shows how different characters react to the things you say and do. One of the best parts of the game is the relationship between Lee and Clementine because their story is emotional from start to finish and the voice acting makes every moment feel real. I love the survival theme of the game and how dangerous the world feels the entire time, especially with the walkers always being a threat and the human characters sometimes being even worse. The story is honestly one of the greatest stories I’ve seen in gaming and even though I won’t spoil too much, the ending of Season 1 is one of the saddest and most emotional endings ever made in a video game. The game perfectly shows how much Lee cares about Clementine and how their journey changes both of them throughout the apocalypse. I also love how the later seasons continue Clementine’s story and show her growing up and becoming stronger after everything she went through in the first game. The extras and choice system make the game really replayable because you can go back and change decisions to see different outcomes and character reactions. Overall The Walking Dead is an emotional, intense and unforgettable experience with amazing storytelling, characters and atmosphere, and it is easily one of the best story games ever made in my opinion.
great game, loved it, so much emotion and dynamics are great, lee and clementine was such a great duo and the whole season was just peak, i reccommend this game to story game lovers.
Игра крутая, но нужно играть без интернета, т.к. не получается подключится к серверам
absolute brutal, very hard decisions to make and emotional moments are very heavy and it makes you realize that your decisions will have consequences.

A beautiful narrative masterpiece from start to finish. You really feel the weight of the choices you have to make and every character is developed well from their personality, flaws and motives. You will feel frustrated, angry, sad and proud at them as you become immersed into the plot. The journey you go on with Lee and Clementine as you see her grow from the timid girl at the beginning into someone more confident and sure is truly heart-warming and speaks volumes of the connection you develop along the way not just with Clementine but with Lee as well. As he evolves into a role model and father figure, teaching us that we mustn't let our past define who we are but instead allow it to mould us into who we wish to be.
